title: Super high GPU usage when dithering and debanding are enabled
author: bibsp
body: ### mpv Information ```bash mpv v0.41.0-dev-g62f149466 Copyright © 2000-2026 mpv/MPlayer/mplayer2 projects built on Jun 27 2026 21:33:47 libplacebo version: v7.365.0 FFmpeg version: ff59522 FFmpeg library versions: libavcodec 62.28.102 libavdevice 62.3.102 libavfilter 11.14.102 libavformat 62.12.102 libavutil 60.26.102 libswresample 6.3.102 libswscale 9.5.102 ``` ### Other Information ```text - Windows version:Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19045.6466] - GPU model, driver and version:Intel HD graphics 4400, 20.19.15.5171 - Source of mpv:Clang - Latest known working version: - Issue started after the following happened: ``` ### Reproduction Steps There is nothing to reproduce. Player is overall heavy. This is my config: [mpv.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29998240/mpv.zip) There are two problems... 1. Player applies dither even when the source is 8bit. MPC Video Renderer only applies dither on 10bit. 2. 2nd problem is that the dither algorithm is super heavy in mpv. `Ordered` is unusable. It ruins the image. `Fruit` is better but not up to the mark. So I use `Error Diffusion`, which is close to MPC Video Renderer. But the fact is MPCVR does the same job, produces similar quality with much much less GPU resources. Check the images. I played same videos in both players. One is HEVC 10bit 24fps, another one is H264 8bit 60fps. ### Expected Behavior **Efficient playback:** 1. Optimize Debanding and Dithering. Especially Dithering. You can search issue number 273 here: https://github.com/Aleksoid1978/VideoRenderer Use the same algorithm for better efficieny. 2. Activate dithering only when needed. Like 10bit to 8bit. Not always. ### Actual Behavior Heavy GPU usage ### Log File [output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29999062/output.txt) ### Sample Files _No response_ ### I carefully read all instruction and confirm that I did the following: - [x] I tested and confirmed that the issue exists with the [latest release version](https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/releases/latest) or newer. - [x] I provided all required information including system and mpv version. - [x] I produced the log file with the exact same set of files, parameters, and conditions used in "Reproduction Steps", with the addition of `--log-file=output.txt`. - [x] I produced the log file while the behaviors described in "Actual Behavior" were actively observed. - [x] I attached the full, untruncated log file. - [x] I attached the backtrace in the case of a crash.

title: Bug report: libmpv OpenGL render API — INVALID_ENUM / black hardware‑decoded video with subtitles (nvidia‑open)
author: slimture
body:
### mpv Information

```bash
mpv v0.41.0 Copyright © 2000-2025 mpv/MPlayer/mplayer2 projects
 built on Feb 11 2026 22:07:06
libplacebo version: v7.360.1
FFmpeg version: n8.0.1 (runtime n8.1.2)
FFmpeg library versions:
   libavcodec      62.11.100 (runtime 62.28.102)
   libavdevice     62.1.100 (runtime 62.3.102)
   libavfilter     11.4.100 (runtime 11.14.102)
   libavformat     62.3.100 (runtime 62.12.102)
   libavutil       60.8.100 (runtime 60.26.102)
   libswresample   6.1.100 (runtime 6.3.102)
   libswscale      9.1.100 (runtime 9.5.102)
```

### Other Information

```text
- Linux version: NAME="Arch Linux" PRETTY_NAME="Arch Linux"
- Kernel Version: Linux slimarch 7.1.3-arch1-2 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 09 Jul 2026 19:55:55 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
- GPU Model: 09:00.0 VGA compatible controller [0300]: NVIDIA Corporation AD104 [GeForce RTX 4070 SUPER] [10de:2783] (rev a1)
- Mesa/GPU Driver Version: OpenGL version string: 4.6.0 NVIDIA 610.43.03
- Window Manager and Version:
- Source of mpv:
- Latest known working version:
- Issue started after the following happened:
```

### Reproduction Steps

This reproduces only through the **libmpv OpenGL render API** rendering into an
externally-supplied GL context (Qt `QOpenGLWidget`), so a `--no-config`
standalone run does **not** apply. A ~190-line PyQt6 + libmpv harness is the
minimal case (attached under *Sample Files*):

```
# reproduces (hardware decode + a subtitle -> artifacts/black, subtitle still draws):
python3 hwdec_sub_test.py FILE --minimal --sid 3 --seek 600 --gpu-debug 2>gpu-debug.log

# forcing a desktop OpenGL 4.6 context instead of GLES -> STILL fails:
python3 hwdec_sub_test.py FILE --minimal --sid 3 --seek 600 --gl

# software decode -> works:
python3 hwdec_sub_test.py FILE --minimal --sid 3 --seek 600 --hwdec no
```

The harness builds `MpvRenderContext(mpv, "opengl", ...)` on a bare
`QOpenGLWidget`, plays the file, and enables the first non-forced subtitle. FILE
is a 10-bit HEVC (HDR, `p010`, `bt.2020/pq`) with an embedded SubRip track.

**Standalone mpv does NOT reproduce** — same file, subtitle and hwdec render
correctly with **both `--vo=gpu` and `--vo=gpu-next`**:

```
mpv --no-config --vo=gpu      --hwdec=auto-copy-safe --sid=3 --start=600 FILE   # OK
mpv --no-config --vo=gpu-next --hwdec=auto-copy-safe --sid=3 --start=600 FILE   # OK
```

Every hardware backend fails identically through the render API:
`auto-copy-safe` (→ `vulkan-copy`), `nvdec`, `nvdec-copy`, `auto-safe`.
Reproduces at 1080p as well as 4K.

### Expected Behavior

Hardware-decoded video composited with a subtitle through the OpenGL render API
renders correctly (as it does under `--vo=gpu`/`--vo=gpu-next`), or the render
API selects a texture format the driver accepts.

### Actual Behavior

The hardware-decoded video plane turns to artifacts / black the moment a subtitle
is composited, while the subtitle itself keeps drawing. mpv logs `INVALID_ENUM`
on texture creation for both the video plane and the OSD (subtitle) texture.

GLES context (Qt's default on Wayland/NVIDIA):

```
libmpv_render: GL_VERSION='OpenGL ES 3.2 NVIDIA 610.43.03'
libmpv_render: Detected GLES 3.2.
Using hardware decoding (vulkan-copy).
vo/libmpv: reconfig to 3840x2160 p010 bt.2020-ncl/bt.2020/pq/limited/display
libmpv_render: Texture for plane 0: 3840x2160
libmpv_render: after creating texture: OpenGL error INVALID_ENUM.   <-- video
libmpv_render: Reallocating OSD texture to 256x128.
libmpv_render: after creating texture: OpenGL error INVALID_ENUM.   <-- subtitle
```

Forced desktop OpenGL 4.6 context + `nvdec-copy` — same failure:

```
libmpv_render: GL_VERSION='4.6.0 NVIDIA 610.43.03'
libmpv_render: Detected desktop OpenGL 4.6.
Using hardware decoding (nvdec-copy).
libmpv_render: Texture for plane 0: 3840x2160
libmpv_render: after creating texture: OpenGL error INVALID_ENUM.
libmpv_render: Reallocating OSD texture to 256x128.
libmpv_render: after creating texture: OpenGL error INVALID_ENUM.
```

With `--gpu-debug` the error brackets video rendering / texture creation itself:

```
libmpv_render: after video rendering: OpenGL error INVALID_ENUM.
libmpv_render: before video texture creation: OpenGL error INVALID_ENUM.
```

Note: `INVALID_ENUM` is also logged with `hwdec=no`, where playback works — so
the error alone is not fatal, but with hardware decoding the video plane texture
is affected and the picture is lost.

### Log File

[gpu-debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29993365/gpu-debug.log)

### Sample Files

- The reproduction harness: `hwdec_sub_test.py` (~190 lines, PyQt6 + libmpv only)
  — in the dopeIPTV repo at `tools/hwdec_sub_test.py`
  (<https://github.com/slimture/dopeIPTV/blob/main/tools/hwdec_sub_test.py>).

### I carefully read all instruction and confirm that I did the following:

- [x] I tested and confirmed that the issue exists with the [latest release version](https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/releases/latest) or newer.
- [x] I provided all required information including system and mpv version.
- [x] I produced the log file with the exact same set of files, parameters, and conditions used in "Reproduction Steps", with the addition of `--log-file=output.txt`.
- [x] I produced the log file while the behaviors described in "Actual Behavior" were actively observed.
- [x] I attached the full, untruncated log file.
- [x] I attached the backtrace in the case of a crash.

title: Crash when using vulkan hardware decoding with ffv1 codec
author: MK73DS
body:
### mpv Information

```bash
mpv v0.41.0 Copyright © 2000-2025 mpv/MPlayer/mplayer2 projects
 built on Feb 11 2026 22:07:06
libplacebo version: v7.360.1
FFmpeg version: n8.0.1 (runtime n8.1.2)
FFmpeg library versions:
   libavcodec      62.11.100 (runtime 62.28.102)
   libavdevice     62.1.100 (runtime 62.3.102)
   libavfilter     11.4.100 (runtime 11.14.102)
   libavformat     62.3.100 (runtime 62.12.102)
   libavutil       60.8.100 (runtime 60.26.102)
   libswresample   6.1.100 (runtime 6.3.102)
   libswscale      9.1.100 (runtime 9.5.102)
```

### Other Information

```text
- Linux version: Arch Linux
- Kernel Version: Linux [PC NAME] 7.1.3-arch1-2 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 09 Jul 2026 19:55:55 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
- GPU Model: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. [AMD/ATI] Rembrandt [Radeon 680M]
- Mesa/GPU Driver Version: OpenGL version string: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 26.2.0-devel (git-f2d39008f9)
- Window Manager and Version: KDE Plasma Version: 6.7.2
- Source of mpv: Arch repository
- Latest known working version: None
- Issue started after the following happened: None
```

### Reproduction Steps

1. Create/get a video encoded with the ffv1 codec, for example `ffmpeg -f lavfi -t 10 -i testsrc=s=1920x1080:r=60,format=yuv420p -c:v ffv1 -an test.mkv`
2. run mpv with hardware acceleration set to vulkan (or auto) `mpv --no-config --hwdec=vulkan test.mkv`

### Expected Behavior

The video should play fine and there shouldn't be a crash. This behavior is attainable by not using hardware acceleration : `mpv --no-config --hwdec=no test.mkv`.
Moreover, if this format is not supported by hardware acceleration, it should not try and use it when `--hwdec` is set to `auto`.

### Actual Behavior

The programs hangs for a few seconds, then a green screen appears in the video player alongside lots of errors in the console. The video never plays and mpv has to be forced to exit using `ctrl+c` in the terminal. This even happens when `--hwdec` is set to `auto`.

### Log File

Log file after running `mpv --no-config --hwdec=vulkan --gpu-debug --log-file=log.txt test.mkv` [log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29935770/log.txt)

### Sample Files

Any video encoded with ffv1 triggers this issue, so creating sample files using ffmpeg is easy. In any case, here is the `test.mkv` file I used to submit this issue. https://files.catbox.moe/63xh8k.mkv

### I carefully read all instruction and confirm that I did the following:

- [x] I tested and confirmed that the issue exists with the [latest release version](https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/releases/latest) or newer.
- [x] I provided all required information including system and mpv version.
- [x] I produced the log file with the exact same set of files, parameters, and conditions used in "Reproduction Steps", with the addition of `--log-file=output.txt`.
- [x] I produced the log file while the behaviors described in "Actual Behavior" were actively observed.
- [x] I attached the full, untruncated log file.
- [x] I attached the backtrace in the case of a crash.

title: Curl backend needs a way to force ipv4/ipv6
author: chcore
body:
### Expected behavior of the wanted feature

The  curl backend needs a way to force ipv4/ipv6.

Sometimes, youtube blocks a host's ipv6 address but allows its ipv4 address, or vice versa. We can force `yt-dlp` to use ipv4 or ipv6 with `--ytdl-raw-options` and get the stream URLs, but it does not look like there's a way to force a specific stack for the retrieval.

I'm currenty getting 403 errors because while yt-dlp gets the URLs correctly (ipv4), mpv tries to play the content using ipv6.

### Alternative behavior of the wanted feature

Some method of forcing ipv4 or ipv6. Maybe even a `--curl-raw-options` argument.

### Log File

_No response_

### Sample Files

_No response_

title: Infinite contrast with ICC profile without black point data
author: yZibi
body: ### mpv Information ```bash mpv v0.41.0 Copyright © 2000-2025 mpv/MPlayer/mplayer2 projects built on Dec 25 2025 15:27:46 libplacebo version: v7.358.0 (v7.351.0-106-gbc90ef9-dirty) FFmpeg version: N-122267-gabb152413 FFmpeg library versions: libavcodec 62.23.102 libavdevice 62.2.100 libavfilter 11.10.101 libavformat 62.8.101 libavutil 60.20.100 libswresample 6.2.100 libswscale 9.3.100 ``` ### Other Information ```text - Windows version: 11 24h2 26100.4061 - GPU model, driver and version: GTX1070, 582.28 - Monitor: Dell h2421hs [SDR] - Source of mpv: winget - vo=gpu-next ``` ### Reproduction Steps Enabling ICC profile makes near-black regions noticeably darker than without it. Tested with Dell factory ICC profile, and with the standard Windows "sRGB IEC61966-2.1" ICC profile. The default (non-ICC) rendering closely matches madVR configured as: - This display is already calibrated - Pure power curve 2.20 The issue is identical with Vulkan and D3D11. The following options made no visible difference: --target-trc=gamma2.2 --icc-use-luma=no --icc-force-contrast=1000 logfile says: [v][vo/gpu-next/libplacebo] Opened ICC profile: Raw black point: X=0.000000% Y=0.000000% Z=-0.000000% Contrast = 203 cd/m^2 : 0.001 mcd/m^2 ≈ 203000000 : 1 Measured primaries: White: x=0.312696, y=0.329020 Red: x=0.649, y=0.336 Green: x=0.310, y=0.618 Blue: x=0.146, y=0.055 Containing primaries: ITU-R Rec. BT.2020 (Ultra HD) Transfer function: IEC 61966-2-4 sRGB (CRT emulation) Even when using: --icc-force-contrast=1000 the log still reports the same contrast value. Q: Is the reported black point of zero and the resulting ~203,000,000:1 contrast expected for a normal SDR ICC profile? P.S. --vo=gpu - no image, just grey screen. --vo=direct3d has no issue (maybe cuz it doesn't load profile?) ### Expected Behavior mpv --no-config Result: - correct black level - visually matches madVR ### Actual Behavior mpv --no-config --icc-profile-auto=yes or mpv --no-config --icc-profile="C:\Windows\System32\spool\drivers\color\sRGB Color Space Profile.icm" Result: - darker near-blacks / shadow detail - visible difference from baseline ### Log File [output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29906891/output.txt) ### I carefully read all instruction and confirm that I did the following: - [x] I tested and confirmed that the issue exists with the [latest release version](https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/releases/latest) or newer. - [x] I provided all required information including system and mpv version. - [x] I produced the log file with the exact same set of files, parameters, and conditions used in "Reproduction Steps", with the addition of `--log-file=output.txt`. - [x] I produced the log file while the behaviors described in "Actual Behavior" were actively observed. - [x] I attached the full, untruncated log file.

title: mpv hangs when playing livestream with vulkan, `--profile=high-quality` and `gpu-next`
author: Axiom415
body:
### mpv Information

```bash
mpv v0.41.0-dev-g115b87b52 Copyright © 2000-2026 mpv/MPlayer/mplayer2 projects
 built on Jul  9 2026 03:25:37
libplacebo version: v7.371.0
FFmpeg version: ff59522
FFmpeg library versions:
   libavcodec      62.28.102
   libavdevice     62.3.102
   libavfilter     11.14.102
   libavformat     62.12.102
   libavutil       60.26.102
   libswresample   6.3.102
   libswscale      9.5.102
```

### Other Information

```text
- Windows version: Windows 11 25H2 Build 26200.8737
- GPU model, driver and version:
-NVIDIA GeForce 920 MX, version 582.28
- Source of mpv: Github CI
- Latest known working version: -
- Issue started after the following happened: Trying to watch Youtube livestream
```

### Reproduction Steps

Requires very specific setup. It must be a one-off livestream (not those 24 hours ones), `--vo=gpu-next`, `--gpu-api=vulkan`, and `--profile=high-quality`.
Other than that it works everywhere. Tried `d3d11`, without profile, `vo=gpu`, even tried with those 24/7 livestreams, all worked.

### Expected Behavior

Should be working.

### Actual Behavior

When starting mpv, it starts windowed. Then after the livestream is playing, attempting to make it full screen will hang after around 5 seconds, no response at all.

### Log File

[output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29856545/output.txt)

### Sample Files

_No response_

### I carefully read all instruction and confirm that I did the following:

title: Decoding using NVDEC pin's the memory frequency to max even in idle
author: TerohsLab
body:
### mpv Information

```bash
Current : https://flathub.org/en/apps/io.mpv.Mpv
```

### Other Information

```text
Operating System: openSUSE Tumbleweed 20260703
KDE Plasma Version: 6.7.2
KDE Frameworks Version: 6.27.0
Qt Version: 6.11.1
Kernel Version: 7.1.2-1-default (64-bit)
Graphics Platform: Wayland
Processors: 8 × Intel® Xeon® CPU E3-1231 v3 @ 3.40GHz
Memory: 16 GiB of RAM (15.5 GiB usable)
Graphics Processor: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060
```

### Reproduction Steps

Have a config looking like this : 

`hwdec=nvdec,vulkan,auto`

Channel through the HW  accel options with ctrl+h and watch the difference in your preferred system monitor. Mine is ( [Mission Center](https://gitlab.com/mission-center-devs/mission-center) ),

Another unrelated side note : You can never come back to NVDec this way. No matter how hard you cycle with crtl+h, NVDec will only work the first time. Probably demands its own issue.

### Expected Behavior

I expected NVDec to relax down to like 5000 MHZ after the initial load spike, but it never EVER is not using max vRAM frequency.

### Actual Behavior

Every other codec relaxes after the initial spike. NVDec is also the only codec that pulls 34 Watts instead of 20 when playing a 1080p livestream. This is most likely because of the vRAM frequency pinning.

title: custom fps limiter/override for frame-step play/mute
author: CatKasha
body:
### Expected behavior of the wanted feature

as far i understand `frame-step play` just play the video at current speed to the next frame
holding it is kinda fast for regular videos, for really low fps content like gif its actually slower than using `frame-step seek` and single press will be laggy because it "plays" to the next frame
holding `frame-step seek` do it slower but very often with high res and heavier codecs it do it extremely slow (to the point that it is better to play the video at a low speed)

it would be nice to have a custom fps limiter/override option that will play at needed speed and for lower fps content it will speedup until it reach next frame

while searching i also saw this [comment](https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/pull/10132#issuecomment-2617809462) about using backwards playback for backward frame-step, could this be added as experimental option?

### Alternative behavior of the wanted feature

_No response_

### Log File

_No response_

### Sample Files

_No response_

title: Reproducible access violation (NULL+0x2C read) on worker thread during live DASH playback with frequent Period switching
author: annah464545-hub
body:
### mpv Information

```bash
・Reproduced on two independent builds:
　　1.mpv-1.dll bundled with mpv.net
　　2.mpv-2.dll from shinchiro/mpv-winbuild-cmake (dev package, mpv-dev-x86_64-v3-20260524-git-9e06c32)

・Both builds show the file version resource 2.0.0.0 (no further version string embedded)
・Embedded in a Python application via python-mpv (ctypes), libmpv API, vo=gpu-next (default) or vo=gpu (tested both, same result)
```

### Other Information

```text
Windows 10 (build 26100.1), Windows 11-style UI, AMD Ryzen with AMD Radeon(TM) Graphics (integrated), driver 31.0.21925.1001
```

### Reproduction Steps

1.Open a live MPEG-DASH stream (ClearKey-encrypted; decryption is handled by a local HTTP proxy that rewrites the MPD and hands mpv a plain, non-DRM local URL, so mpv itself never touches the ClearKey layer)

2.Let it play through several minutes of content, including multiple "commercial break" segments

3.The commercial-break portion of this particular stream is unusual: it publishes a new <Period> element roughly every 15–30 seconds (see attached manifest_1783409233627.mpd, which shows three Periods only 15s and 30s apart, none of them encrypted)

4.After some number of these rapid Period transitions (not perfectly deterministic, but reliably reproducible within a few minutes of playback), the process crashes

### Expected Behavior

The stream should continue playing through the Period transition without crashing.

### Actual Behavior

The process crashes with an access violation (0xc0000005, read from NULL+0x2C) after playing
through several rapid <Period> transitions in a live MPEG-DASH stream (a new Period is published
roughly every 15-30 seconds during this stream's ad-break segments; see attached manifest.mpd).

Reproduced identically on two independent builds:
- mpv-1.dll bundled with mpv.net
- mpv-2.dll from shinchiro/mpv-winbuild-cmake (dev package, mpv-dev-x86_64-v3-20260524-git-9e06c32)

Both crash with the same instruction pattern:
  mov r12d, dword ptr [rax+2Ch]   ; rax = 0

The crash happens on a background worker thread (ucrtbase!thread_start), not the main thread.
Reproduced with both vo=gpu and vo=gpu-next, and with hwdec=no (software decoding), so it doesn't
appear to be tied to a specific VO backend or hardware decoding.

Embedded via python-mpv (ctypes/libmpv). Full manifest.mpd from one crash session is attached.

### Log File

[python.exe.14488.dmp](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29736202/python.exe.14488.dmp)

[output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29745963/output.txt)

### Sample Files

[manifest_1783409233627.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29736325/manifest_1783409233627.txt)

title: [D3D11VA] Request to enable DXVA HEVC YUV444 support
author: AndrewKeYanzhe
body:

```bash

```

### Other Information

```text
- Windows version: Windows 11 25H2
- GPU model, driver and version: RTX 2080
- Source of mpv:
- Latest known working version: NIL
- Issue started after the following happened:
```

### Reproduction Steps

Play a HEVC YUV 444 video (10 bit for example). Notice that it is software decoded.

### Expected Behavior

Hardware decoding works.

### Actual Behavior

Hardware decoding doesn't work.

As @kasper93 explained in https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/issues/18172#issuecomment-4793213696 :

> 444 HEVC is supported in official documentation now https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/ddi/dxva/ns-dxva-dxva_picparams_hevc_rangeext 

> I could implement this official extension, but I don't have hardware to test this on, so I won't.

I am willing to try and implement the fix, allowing MPV to utilise D3D11VA's HEVC YUV444 hardware decoder. I would like to get the HEVC 444 8b, 10b and 12b variants all working.

I would greatly appreciate if any MPV developer can provide some guidance, including which files I should look at/edit.

If any developer wants to help and implement the fix but lacks the hardware to test it on, I am more than happy to help run the tests. I have an RTX 2080.

  Metadata:
    major_brand     : isom

    compatible_brands: isomiso2avc1mp41
    encoder         : Lavf56.40.101
  Duration: 00:10:17.68, start: 0.000000, bitrate: 1591 kb/s

      handler_name    : SoundHandler
      vendor_id       : [0][0][0][0]
```

dpkg -S libcuda.so.1
`dpkg-query: no path found matching pattern *libcuda.so.1*`

apt-file search libcuda.so.1
```
libnvidia-compute-470: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
libnvidia-compute-470-server: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1

no "Cannot load" noise for things which are not used/needed, which do not need attention

it makes layman user think that there is a issue, wasting time

Play any video file with `mpv --no-config` 2. Use `screenshot` and `screenshot window` commands to take `.jpg` screenshots. 3. Set `mpv --screenshot-format=png` and repeat `screenshot` and `screenshot window` commands to get the corresponding `.png` screenshots. ### Expected Behavior All screenshots should have the same brightness as those taken using the mpv builds from before `mpv-x86_64-20260627-git-62f1494661`. ### Actual Behavior This is taken using `screenshot` with the default `mpv`: `screenshot` with `mpv --screenshot-format=png`: `screenshot window` with `mpv`: `screenshot window` with `mpv --screenshot-format=png`: As a reference, this screenshot was taken at fullscreen using ShareX; the `.png` taken using the `screenshot` command is the only one of the four that is close to it in terms of brightness: Using the `mpv-x86_64-20260627-git-1cf081e66c` build, I get the following: `screenshot` with `mpv`: `screenshot` with `mpv --screenshot-format=png`: `screenshot window` with `mpv`: `screenshot window` with `mpv --screenshot-format=png`: With this build, all versions of the screenshots taken are the same brightness and they match the screenshot taken of my monitor using ShareX. ### Log File [log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29613756/log.txt) ### Sample Files Any video file should work to reproduce the issue. ### I carefully read all instruction and confirm that I did the following: - [x] I tested and confirmed that the issue exists with the [latest release version](https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/releases/latest) or newer. - [x] I provided all required information including system and mpv version. - [x] I produced the log file with the exact same set of files, parameters, and conditions used in "Reproduction Steps", with the addition of `--log-file=output.txt`. - [x] I produced the log file while the behaviors described in "Actual Behavior" were actively observed. - [x] I attached the full, untruncated log file. - [x] I attached the backtrace in the case of a crash.

### Reproduction Steps

```
% mpv --no-config MyMovie.mkv
● Video  --vid=1  --vlang=en-US  'MyMovie' (av1 1920x1080 24 fps) [default]
● Audio  --aid=1  --alang=fr-FR  'Français VFF' (eac3 6ch 48000 Hz) [default]
○ Audio  --aid=2  --alang=en-US  'Anglais VO' (eac3 6ch 48000 Hz)
○ Subs   --sid=1  --slang=fr-FR  'Français Forcés' (subrip) [default forced]
○ Subs   --sid=2  --slang=fr-FR  'Français' (subrip)
○ Subs   --sid=3  --slang=fr-FR  'Français SDH' (subrip) [hearing-impaired]
● Subs   --sid=4  --slang=en-US  'Anglais VO SDH' (subrip) [hearing-impaired]
File tags:
 Title: MyMovie
AO: [pipewire] 48000Hz 5.1(side) 6ch floatp
VO: [gpu-next] 1920x1080 yuv420p10
AV: 00:00:00 / 00:00:05 (5%) A-V:  0.000 Cache: 4.5s/820KB
```

### Expected Behavior

The first (and default) subtitle track should be selected.

### Actual Behavior

The last subtitle track is selected.

If I use "mpv --no-config --sid=1 MyMovie.mkv", sid=1 is selected.

If all subtracks are in slang=xx or slang=xxx format ("fr", "en" or "fre"), there is no issue. Issue is here with slang=xx-XX ("fr-FR" or "en-US) format only.

1. If all subtracks are in slang=xx format except the first (and default) one, the second will be selected.
2. If all subtracks are in slang=xx format except the first and second, the third will be selected, etc etc…
3. When all subtracks are in slang=xx-XX format, the last is always selected.

### Log File

[output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29598180/output.txt)

### Sample Files

[MyMovie.mkv.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29598714/MyMovie.mkv.zip)

You can also find the video sample here:
https://homer.krusty.eu.org/MyMovie.mkv

title: Continuous memory growth (heap, not GPU memory) when repeatedly calling mpv_render_context_render() into an app-owned FBO via libmpv OpenGL Render API on Windows
author: kuiro1024
body:
### mpv Information

```bash
mpv v0.41.0 Copyright © 2000-2025 mpv/MPlayer/mplayer2 projects
 built on May  9 2026 22:23:39
libplacebo version: v7.360.1
FFmpeg version: 8.1.1 (runtime 8.1.2)
FFmpeg library versions:
   libavcodec      62.28.101 (runtime 62.28.102)
   libavdevice     62.3.101 (runtime 62.3.102)
   libavfilter     11.14.101 (runtime 11.14.102)
   libavformat     62.12.101 (runtime 62.12.102)
   libavutil       60.26.101 (runtime 60.26.102)
   libswresample   6.3.101 (runtime 6.3.102)
   libswscale      9.5.101 (runtime 9.5.102)
```

### Other Information

```text
- Windows version: Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.26200.8655]
- GPU model, driver and version: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070, driver 591.86
- Source of the mpv binary: MSYS2 package `mingw-w64-x86_64-mpv` (0.41.0-4, latest available at time of testing)
- Latest known working version: N/A (not tested against a different mpv version; issue is present in the only version we've used)
- Issue started after the following happened: N/A (first observed during our own libmpv Render API integration work, not tied to a specific mpv/driver/system update)
```

### Reproduction Steps

Note: This is a libmpv embedding (Render API) issue, not reproducible via plain `mpv.exe` CLI playback — it requires driving `mpv_render_context_render()` from host application code. A minimal C/C++ repro project can be provided if useful; steps below describe the integration pattern we used.

1. Embed libmpv via the Render API (`MPV_RENDER_API_TYPE_OPENGL`, `MPV_RENDER_PARAM_ADVANCED_CONTROL=1`) inside a Qt `QOpenGLWidget` (desktop GL, not ANGLE).
2. Create a single FBO + texture once (fixed size, e.g. 480x270), and re-use it every frame (never recreated, same GL texture id and FBO id for the whole playback session).
3. On the render-context update callback, mark a "frame dirty" flag. On the next paint (driven by the update callback, not a fixed timer), if dirty:
   - call `mpv_render_context_update()`
   - if the returned flags include `MPV_RENDER_UPDATE_FRAME`, call `mpv_render_context_render()` with:
     - `MPV_RENDER_PARAM_OPENGL_FBO` pointing at the reused FBO (`mpv_opengl_fbo{ fbo, w, h, internal_format = GL_RGBA8 }`)
     - `MPV_RENDER_PARAM_FLIP_Y = 0`
     - `MPV_RENDER_PARAM_BLOCK_FOR_TARGET_TIME = 0`
   - then call `mpv_render_context_report_swap()`
4. Play any local H.264/AAC .mp4 file continuously (tested with 640x360 @ 23.976fps).
5. Watch the process's Private Bytes / Commit Size (Task Manager "Memory (private working set)", or `PROCESS_MEMORY_COUNTERS_EX.PrivateUsage`) over several minutes.

Options used:
hwdec=no
vd-lavc-dr=no
opengl-pbo=no   (tested both yes/no, no effect on the growth)
gpu-api=opengl
vo=libmpv
ao=wasapi
cache=yes

Isolation steps we performed (see "Actual Behavior" for results of each):
A. hwdec=auto-safe (→ hwdec-current=d3d11va-copy)
B. hwdec=no (confirmed hwdec-current=no) + vd-lavc-dr=no
C. Same as B, plus opengl-pbo=no
D. Same as B, but the app skips its own call to mpv_render_context_render() entirely for several minutes while decoding/audio continue normally (vid=auto, pause=no) — i.e. video is intentionally never rendered/presented, only decoded
E. Same as B, but with mpv_render_context_update() correctly called and MPV_RENDER_UPDATE_FRAME checked before every render() call (our original integration never called mpv_render_context_update() at all)
F. Standalone `mpv.exe` (same MSYS2 package, same file, --hwdec=no) playing via its own normal window (not the Render API)

### Expected Behavior

Process memory (Private Bytes / Commit Size) should stay roughly stable during continuous playback, similar to what we observe with case D (render() calls skipped) and case F (standalone mpv.exe), which both plateau after a short warm-up period.

### Actual Behavior

Process memory (Private Bytes / Commit Size) grows continuously and without bound for as long as playback continues, at roughly 13-25 MB/min depending on hwdec, with no plateau observed even after 15+ minutes of continuous playback of the same file. See attached log for a representative run (case B in the Reproduction Steps: hwdec=no, vd-lavc-dr=no, opengl-pbo=no, mpv_render_context_update() correctly called) showing PrivateUsage growing from 373MB to 454MB over ~375 seconds (~13.0 MB/min), linear with no sign of leveling off.

Summary of the isolation work we did (full detail in the "Reproduction Steps" section above):
- Not specific to hwdec=auto-safe/d3d11va-copy: switching to hwdec=no (confirmed via hwdec-current=no) shows the same growth rate.
- Not the OpenGL PBO upload path: opengl-pbo=no has no effect.
- Not decoding/demuxing/caching: if the host app simply stops calling mpv_render_context_render() (while decode/audio continue normally), memory plateaus after a short warm-up. Calling render() is necessary and sufficient to reproduce the growth.
- Not caused by skipping mpv_render_context_update(): adding it (and checking MPV_RENDER_UPDATE_FRAME) made no difference.
- Not GPU VRAM / driver-side retention: VMMap shows the growth is entirely in the process's own heap ("Heap" category), while GPU-driver-managed memory ("Private Data") stays flat.
- Standalone mpv.exe playing the same file normally (own window, not the Render API) does not show this growth.

This strongly suggests the leak is somewhere in the OpenGL Render API's internal per-frame rendering path (used when repeatedly calling mpv_render_context_render() to draw into an app-provided FBO), rather than in decoding, caching, or GPU-driver-managed resources.

We noted `Using FBO format rgba16f` in the verbose logs (mpv's internal working format for the render pass chain), while our target FBO uses `internal_format = GL_RGBA8` — we don't know if a format mismatch here is relevant, but flagging it in case it's a useful lead.

### Log File

[log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29576951/log.txt)

### Sample Files

_No response_

### I carefully read all instruction and confirm that I did the following:

- [x] I tested and confirmed that the issue exists with the [latest release version](https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/releases/latest) or newer.
- [x] I provided all required information including system and mpv version.
- [x] I produced the log file with the exact same set of files, parameters, and conditions used in "Reproduction Steps", with the addition of `--log-file=output.txt`.
- [x] I produced the log file while the behaviors described in "Actual Behavior" were actively observed.
- [x] I attached the full, untruncated log file.
- [x] I attached the backtrace in the case of a crash.

title: MPV on Vulkan becomes transparent when Nvidia overlay notification shows up
author: dabiOW
body:
### mpv Information

```bash
mpv v0.41.0-826-g5abce15f1 Copyright © 2000-2026 mpv/MPlayer/mplayer2 projects
 built on Jun 28 2026 16:02:32
libplacebo version: v7.365.0 (v7.360.0-90-g50a7571-dirty)
FFmpeg version: N-125338-g97115451d
FFmpeg library versions:
   libavcodec      63.2.100
   libavdevice     63.2.100
   libavfilter     12.2.100
   libavformat     63.2.100
   libavutil       61.2.100
   libswresample   7.2.100
   libswscale      10.2.100
```

### Other Information

```text
- Windows version: Windows 11 25H2
- GPU model, driver and version: Geforce RTX 5080, driver 610.62
- Source of mpv: zhongfly mpv
- Latest known working version: not sure
- Issue started after the following happened:
```

### Reproduction Steps

1. set mpv to gpu-api=vulkan and hwdec=vulkan
2. have the nvidia app installed with nvidia overlay turned on (it's on by default)
3. launch a video in mpv and go fullscreen

### Expected Behavior

dont be transparent

### Actual Behavior

is transparent

### Log File

[output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29443198/output.txt)

### Sample Files

I have a weird bug, where if I set my MPV to use vulkan for hw decoding, the player becomes completely transparent when the "Press alt+v to open Nvidia overlay" ribbon briefly shows up. I recorded a video below, where you can see it plays fine while windowed, but disappears when I go fullscreen. Obviously it's easily fixable by either disabling the Nvidia overlay or using d3d11va api, but ideally it wouldn't happen in the first place.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6623b65c-c2f6-4e5f-a4eb-1f69a90de35f
